method: frames-as-images with a Pillow 't=Ns' overlay per frame (this ffmpeg build lacks drawtext); Cosmos-Reason2 grounds by reading timestamps drawn on the frames
prompt: Each frame has a timestamp 't=Ns' at the bottom-left, sampled in order from a 330-second titration video. First reason briefly about the procedure. Then identify the MAIN distinct steps: group consecutive frames that show the same action (there are roughly 6 to 12 steps, NOT one per frame). For each step give a short DESCRIPTIVE label of what is being done (e.g. 'rinse burette with acid'), and its start and end time in seconds read from the on-screen timestamps. End with ONLY a valid JSON array, each element an object with braces: [{"step": "description", "start": <seconds>, "end": <seconds>}, ...].
